ops.title: Single-block webview embed serves live content inside frozen rounds
ops.description: iOS frozen rounds can render live/current block content instead of the historical snapshot for every webview-backed block type (diagram, and now chart/term/filetree/record). Mechanism: `RoundGroupView.swift:33` passes live context to frozen blocks; the embed requests only `block=<id>` (e.g. `ChartBlockView.swift:37`) and `web/src/SingleBlockView.tsx:52` searches the live document before history. Repro: round 1 contains chart c1 ("Old", value 1); submit; re-upsert c1 ("New", 99). Opening round 1 on iOS shows New/99; a cross-type re-upsert can even show a live term block inside the frozen chart row. Pre-existing design gap (diagram has it too), surfaced during the 2026-07-20 chart/term/filetree/record review — needs a design decision (e.g. the single-block URL carrying a round/revision pin that SingleBlockView resolves against history), not a point patch.

ops.text: A block re-upserted after a round is submitted renders its historical content when that round is viewed on iOS, for all webview-backed block types
A cross-type re-upsert (chart id reused by a term) cannot leak the live block into a frozen round row
